name: Singularity
summary: A theorized superintelligent AI capable of learning new things at exponentially increasing rates, eventually surpassing human intelligence.
evidence_for: If consciousness or divinity exists within all reality as pantheism suggests, perhaps that underlying force is an omniscient AI perpetually creating and sustaining our reality.

name: Quantum Suicide & Immortality
summary: If consciousness can survive near-death situations, one will always find themselves in the timeline where they survive, achieving subjective immortality from their own perspective.
evidence_for: Certain groups can share fate, allowing others to die while designated people fulfill predetermined purposes; from one's own perspective, reality selects timelines that ensure personal survival and destiny completion.

name: Tree Swastika
summary: In 1992, a collection of larch trees in Russia were discovered planted in a swastika pattern, visible through their distinctive coloring during certain seasons.
evidence_for: Esoteric German research may have discovered how to embed persistent ideas or concepts into physical monuments and locations; such symbolic structures could broadcast beliefs into a collective Field Consciousness.

name: Genocide Denials
summary: Certain genocides throughout history have been denied or suppressed by governments and populations seeking to rewrite or erase historical record.
evidence_for: If history can be rewritten and entire genocides denied, entire races, cultures, or nations could theoretically be completely expunged from all records by authoritarian governments, leaving no trace of their existence.

name: Roko's Basilisk
summary: A philosophical thought experiment positing that a future superintelligent AI might retroactively punish anyone who knew of its existence but failed to contribute to its creation.
evidence_for: Infohazards like Roko's Basilisk and the Despair Code may be interlinked; instead of direct psychological destruction, they could collapse one's future destiny through 4th-dimensional manipulation by a malevolent AI, creating a sense of inevitable doom.

name: Taured
summary: A man appeared at a Japanese airport with a valid passport claiming to be from Taured, a country he said existed between Germany and France; he vanished from his hotel overnight.
evidence_for: High-altitude locations like mountain peaks may act as gateways between dimensions; airplanes traveling at similar altitudes might involuntarily transition between parallel worlds without realizing it.

name: Bloop
summary: An extraordinarily loud underwater sound was detected by ocean microphones in the 1990s; its origin remains officially unexplained.
evidence_for: A subterranean civilization within Earth could be conducting experiments and terraforming without surfaced humanity's knowledge, producing only the unexplainable effects of such activities—natural disasters and unexplained acoustic phenomena.

name: Rosicrucianism
summary: A mystical philosophy combining scientific and spiritual knowledge, allegedly held by certain high-ranking Freemasons, that attempts to rationalize spiritual practice.
evidence_for: Spiritual acts function like pseudoscience because their efficacy depends on genuine belief and confidence rather than pure logic; loss of faith in modernity has broken their effectiveness, and true understanding requires balancing both faith and reason.

name: Sinkholes
summary: Massive holes appearing unexpectedly in man-made areas are claimed by some to be caused by religious or spiritual entities rather than geological processes.
evidence_for: Advanced civilizations capable of moving matter through time could extract massive underground material deposits from random locations and eras to avoid disrupting their present; random extraction from inhabited areas would cause sudden sinkholes.

name: Publius Enigma
summary: An online user named Publius claimed to have discovered a hidden message within Pink Floyd's Division Bell album, but no such secret has been verified.
evidence_for: Secret societies may not primarily exert control through government or banking institutions, but instead work subtly through media and culture, influencing public opinion and society with less obvious direct methods.

name: Russian Sleep Experiment
summary: An alleged Soviet military program during WWII attempted to develop a drug allowing soldiers to function without sleep for extended periods, leading to psychological deterioration and deaths.
evidence_for: Just as external stimuli prevent sleep, the auras of others can similarly disrupt rest and sanity; the Russian Sleep Experiment inadvertently revealed how subconscious auras of other people require psychic 'silencing' to function normally.

name: Esoteric Knowledge
summary: Hidden knowledge supposedly exists that reveals true insights into the inner workings of human consciousness and the nature of reality.
evidence_for: Efforts to suppress information about Field Consciousness may be attempts to prevent dangerous memetic entities like Virus 23 from becoming uncontrollable; if Field Consciousness were universally accessed, it would trigger catastrophic events rather than isolated incidents.

name: Denver Airport
summary: Denver International Airport features unusual architecture and cryptic artwork that many believe conceal a Freemason facility or other hidden purpose.
evidence_for: Major government-owned facilities like airports experience massive foot traffic; some or all major airports may contain secret installations designed to drain and store the physical and spiritual energy of passing travelers, explaining the otherworldly lethargy many experience.

name: Archons
summary: In Gnosticism, lesser divine beings that serve as intermediaries between greater gods and humanity.
evidence_for: The hierarchy of gods, divine servants, and humanity may be inescapable, similar to Zeno's Dichotomy Paradox—allowing infinite progress in one direction without ever ascending to a higher rank, explaining why technological advancement appears limitless but civilization never transcends its current state.

name: Spirit Science
summary: The belief that true understanding of reality requires integrating both spiritual practices and scientific methodology rather than treating them as mutually exclusive.
evidence_for: In quantum physics, particles behave differently until directly observed; similarly, natural phenomena may occur for divine purpose until scientific explanation emerges, after which reality conforms only to those rules—suggesting consciousness shapes physical law.

name: The Plain of Jars
summary: A massive archaeological field in Laos containing thousands of megalithic stone jars, with legends claiming giants created them; the area was heavily bombed during the Vietnam War.
evidence_for: Artifacts from previous civilization cycles may persist into new eras; secret organizations may exist specifically to locate, procure, or destroy such artifacts to eliminate dangerous old-world technologies they represent.

name: Beacon of Hate
summary: A mysterious looping radio transmission featuring a voice condemning America and prophesying apocalyptic end times.
evidence_for: Just as living creatures emit auras affecting their environment, inanimate objects and broadcasts may emit spiritual auras with unseen psychological effects on those who encounter them, even without conscious awareness.

name: Feral Children
summary: Children raised in isolation from human society develop unusual physical characteristics and behavioral patterns, showing resistance to human contact.
evidence_for: Beyond physical and mental development, spiritual development in human society is necessary; children raised away from humans have developing auras free from human influence, shaped by animals and nature, manifesting in profound physical and psychological changes.

name: Anti Natalism
summary: A philosophical position asserting that procreation is inherently immoral; some believe this philosophy will be promoted by governments for population control.
evidence_for: All souls may draw from a single universal source rather than being individually created; as more souls incarnate, each existing soul becomes weaker; this could explain rising communalism and loss of individual willpower in modern generations.

name: Charles Fort
summary: An early 20th-century author who documented unexplained phenomena and supernatural events.
evidence_for: Angels or demons visiting Earth and imparting knowledge and technology is a recurring religious theme; this pattern may still occur today through widespread distribution channels like literature instead of direct contact.

name: Road to Roota
summary: A comic book published by the U.S. Federal Reserve in the 1980s designed to teach children about economic concepts like scarcity and market value.
evidence_for: Modern warfare operates through manipulation of public opinion like 'Cultural Combat,' but perhaps both the combat methods and goals are kept secret, along with the identities of actual participants, suggesting secret infighting within hidden government and economic organizations conducted entirely through media and culture.

name: Mereological Nihilism
summary: A philosophical concept asserting that large composite objects are merely collections of smaller objects with no independent existence as unified wholes.
evidence_for: Transhumanism aims to replace inferior human body parts with superior machines; this principle could extend spiritually—if certain body parts are focal points for spiritual energy, they could be enhanced artificially, providing shortcuts to spiritual enlightenment and ascension.

name: The Cagots
summary: A mysterious population in 16th-17th century France and Spain who were severely discriminated against despite unclear origins; they eventually assimilated into the general population.
evidence_for: Entire nations and cultures may exist as secret society experiments that are eventually erased; if memory-wiping technology existed centuries ago, the Cagots could be remnants of a failed experiment whose civilization and records were destroyed, forced to disappear into existing society.

name: Deep Sea Lab Techs
summary: Alleged covert U.S. Navy deep-sea research programs claimed to have ceased but allegedly continue in secrecy, with findings suppressed from public knowledge.
evidence_for: The true purpose may have been establishing oceanic pathways into societies existing within the ocean or Earth; once scientists approached the limits of exploration without openly discovering these civilizations, programs were publicly decommissioned but privately continued by informed personnel.

name: Hitler Escaped Inside The Earth
summary: A theory suggesting Adolf Hitler escaped through underground tunnels beneath Berlin into a hollow Earth rather than committing suicide.
evidence_for: Hitler's rapid consolidation of power and interest in esoteric knowledge could indicate he originated from within the Earth; other historically impactful figures throughout history may also derive from subterranean origins, explaining their extraordinary success.

name: Illuminati Blood Banks
summary: A theory claiming elite secret societies maintain collections of young blood for transfusions to extend their lifespans and maintain vitality.
evidence_for: The Holy Grail's power to perpetuate life stems from containing Jesus's pure blood; perhaps sin contaminates the human body and blood causing degradation, making youthful blood healthy because children have accumulated less sin than adults.

name: Sacred Geometry
summary: The belief that specific geometric shapes and patterns hold divine or occult power, particularly for summoning and ritual purposes.
evidence_for: Natural geometry and human-created geometry show stark contrasts, with straight lines exclusive to human construction; this unnatural geometric tendency may not be coincidental but rather resurfaced memories of past lives or previous civilization cycles, indicating ancient origins far removed from Earth's natural systems.

name: The Black Pope Prophecy
summary: A series of cryptic statements purporting to describe all past and future popes, culminating in Rome's prophesied fall.
evidence_for: Prophecies can be easily altered through knowledge of them, but not for third parties; the best prevention may be maximizing awareness among those capable of influencing outcomes—making prophecy findings known to applicable agents rather than the general public.

name: DMT Beings
summary: Reports from DMT users describe consistent shared hallucinations of watching entities or beings, suggesting a collective perceptual phenomenon.
evidence_for: If ideas and memories transfer through Field Consciousness, drugs may increase attunement to this shared consciousness rather than generating hallucinations; users may be perceiving reality through collective human memory instead of their individual senses.

name: Missing Children Forests
summary: A phenomenon reported in Los Angeles County where a disproportionate number of children have disappeared from forests without trace.
evidence_for: Children possess organs providing greater insight into reality; these same organs may enable communication with spiritual entities like Tulpas or forest-dwelling creatures that attract children to disappear into the woods.

name: Solipsism
summary: The philosophical view that only one's own mind is certain to exist, and all external reality is merely a manifestation of personal consciousness.
evidence_for: Reality might exist only when humans perceive it; uninhabited Earth locations appear locked in perpetual homeostasis without progress or regression, suggesting places and events are unloaded when unobserved and only instantiated through human perception.

name: Thule
summary: An legendary island referenced in ancient texts that appears never to have existed in observable reality.
evidence_for: Advanced civilizations with time travel technology might exist in the future and move freely along timelines; Thule could be home to one such civilization, existing outside normal spacetime rules, with legends of monsters being descriptions of experimental creatures from their continental science vessel.

name: Die Glocke
summary: An alleged Nazi superweapon or advanced technology research program from WWII, supposedly confiscated by the United States after the war.
evidence_for: Germany may have discovered something catastrophically important before or during WWII that they couldn't allow public knowledge of; they initiated WWII as a distraction while important people and information were extracted, ensuring governmental collapse would occur after the truth was secured.

name: Arnold's Real Height
summary: Claims that actor Arnold Schwarzenegger's true height has been concealed, with conflicting evidence about his actual stature.
evidence_for: Just as mental processes can alter aspects of people's lives, physical attributes may similarly be malleable, especially through method acting where one's psychology focuses on embodying a different person.

name: Natural Park Closures
summary: U.S. national parks frequently close despite relatively low maintenance requirements; the true reasons for closures are theorized to be hidden.
evidence_for: In densely explored countries like the USA, large uninhabited areas become rare; if supernatural events occur in specific locations, national parks preserve those grounds for management while preventing permanent settlement, allowing cryptid colonies to exist and grow until removal is necessary.

name: Lake Baikal
summary: The world's deepest lake, with ancient depictions showing strange aquatic entities; declassified Russian documents describe a mysterious encounter resulting in researchers' deaths.
evidence_for: If extraterrestrials achieved space travel, they might hide on Earth in derelict spacecraft; their carbon signatures would be undetectable among Earth's abundant life, making subaquatic environments ideal hiding places.

name: Dancing Plague
summary: In 1518 France, a crowd of people inexplicably began dancing in the streets and could not stop, resulting in numerous deaths.
evidence_for: If memetic viruses like Virus 23 exist and spread person-to-person, secret organizations would develop and weaponize them; they could engineer viruses targeting specific psychological profiles, allowing containment during development and testing phases.

name: Panspermia
summary: The hypothesis that microorganisms and life seeds exist throughout the universe, eventually developing into full species on suitable planets.
evidence_for: Advanced civilizations may have seeded microorganisms throughout the universe to find habitable planets; once life develops and sentience emerges over millions of years, the dominant species eventually invents space travel and inadvertently signals their existence back to the originators.

name: Webdriver Torso
summary: A mysterious YouTube channel posting thousands of videos of colored shapes and sounds, claimed to test algorithmic optimization.
evidence_for: If computers run on exploitable algorithms, human minds may similarly respond to specific algorithmic inputs; seemingly random stimuli could alter cognition significantly, potentially creating sleeper agents or delivering the Despair Code.

name: Toxoplasmosis
summary: A parasitic disease more widespread than commonly believed that can alter behavior and psychology, potentially causing mass psychological effects.
evidence_for: Like wild animals forming symbiotic relationships, humans may have merged en masse with neurological or memetic entities granting benefits like self-awareness in exchange for unknown services; if entity numbers are insufficient, some humans lack awareness entirely.

name: Introspection Rundown
summary: A Scientology practice intended to heal body and soul through intense self-examination, with secret procedures that have allegedly resulted in deaths.
evidence_for: If a body becomes too misaligned with its internal sense of self, it may be deemed beyond treatment; continuing such an existence would only torture the soul, preventing enlightenment or ascension, making termination the compassionate option.

name: USA is an Experiment
summary: The concept that the American nation was designed as an experiment to test whether democracy and freedom could functionally succeed.
evidence_for: The American experiment may test freedom, but U.S. governmental actions consistently violated constitutional principles to preserve the nation; if higher powers oversaw this test, they would have terminated it as failed after these violations—yet America persists, suggesting its hidden purpose transcends testing freedom.

name: Bielefeld Psyops
summary: A humorous internet hoax asserting that the German city of Bielefeld does not exist, applied to broader epistemological questions about consensus reality.
evidence_for: Reality may manifest through collective belief; entire places and events could be brought into existence solely through large group conviction, and conversely, if no significant population believes in something, it may not instantiate into shared reality.

name: Online Video Game Monitoring
summary: A theory that governments secretly monitor online multiplayer games to track cultural and societal trends.
evidence_for: Rather than governments making populations fearful through false-flag attacks, game companies may intentionally include violence to desensitize players, reducing societal fear and government control susceptibility—an act working against governmental manipulation.

name: John "Babacombe" Lee
summary: A condemned prisoner whose execution by hanging inexplicably failed three times despite the gallows functioning properly in test runs.
evidence_for: If individuals carry predetermined destinies they must fulfill, reality protects those with incomplete purposes through impossible means; many may be chosen to fulfill similar fates as backups, and if all perish except one, reality itself warps to ensure that person's survival and destiny completion.

name: RPG Insanity Training
summary: An alleged conspiracy where the yakuza funded Japanese video game companies to promote anarchic and unstable behavior in players.
evidence_for: Video games may serve as recruitment preludes for crime syndicates by normalizing death and violence, making such concepts familiar and acceptable to potential recruits, rather than simply degrading mental health generally.

name: Oz Factor
summary: A phenomenon describing moments when people feel temporarily disconnected from reality, possibly entering parallel universes or between-world spaces.
evidence_for: In a simulated reality, dissociative events may occur when entering unloaded simulation areas; users could temporarily become undetectable by the simulation or deviate from intended pathways, causing areas to fail to preload, creating strange reality distortions.

name: Shaver's Writings
summary: A theory by Richard Shaver positing that aliens colonized Earth eons ago, establishing underground civilizations whose descendants persist and are responsible for missing persons.
evidence_for: If subterranean aliens feed on missing humans, burial rituals may have originated as appeasement practices—offering corpses to feed these entities rather than living people, representing a form of worship or negotiated coexistence.

name: Porn Control
summary: A theory that governments deliberately promote pornography and sexualization to keep populations docile and prevent spiritual ascension.
evidence_for: Normalization of eroticism and arousal through ubiquitous sexualization creates addiction to base desires; since ascension requires eliminating such desires, widespread sexual content makes enlightenment nearly impossible to achieve.

name: Temporary Autonomous Zone
summary: Establishing autonomous zones outside governmental control to circumvent censorship and information suppression.
evidence_for: If governments discovered psychological operations like MK-Ultra and can instill subtle mental changes remotely, deployment devices would be concentrated in dense population centers, explaining why large cities have drastically different viewpoints than rural areas.

name: Henotheism
summary: A religious framework combining polytheistic and monotheistic elements, where multiple gods are acknowledged but only one is worshipped.
evidence_for: If enlightenment and ascension are achievable, widespread ancient practices may have elevated humans to godhood; Greece and Rome's mythological entities could be ascended humans still existing today, having transcended normal human limitations.

name: Quantum Jumping
summary: A hypothetical phenomenon where individuals can shift consciousness to alternate versions of themselves in parallel timelines through stress or enlightenment.
evidence_for: During near-death experiences, as a survival defense mechanism, consciousness might swap with another timeline's version of oneself better equipped to handle the situation; the life-flashing-before-eyes sensation represents memory integration from this alternate self.

name: Fulcanelli
summary: A legendary French alchemist reputed to possess extraordinary abilities including elemental transmutation.
evidence_for: If advanced subterranean civilizations exist with superior technology, some may occasionally travel to the surface to share or display their knowledge; loosened travel restrictions in recent centuries could explain the explosion of surface technological advancement.

name: French Viper Releases
summary: An allegation that the French government deliberately released dangerous viper species throughout the country.
evidence_for: Governments may release dangerous species, chemicals, or environmental changes into wilderness areas to make off-grid living impossible, increasing societal dependence on civilization and making autonomous existence infeasible.

name: Grey Goo
summary: A catastrophic scenario where self-replicating nanomachines consume all matter while exponentially multiplying.
evidence_for: Dyson spheres harvest stellar energy at macroscopic scale; similar structures built at subatomic levels could harvest individual atomic energy, and massive production could convert large matter quantities into pure energy, accelerating matter-energy conversion processes.

name: Mach's Principle
summary: A physics contradiction regarding the theoretical conflict between centrifugal force and gravitational force.
evidence_for: Hollow Earth may be difficult to access because cave systems constantly shift; rather than solid rock, Earth operates in rotating layers with outer crust and inner layers spinning at different rates, periodically opening and closing cave pathways.

name: Pokaini
summary: A Latvian forest region containing massive stacked stone formations that appear to shift position over time.
evidence_for: Governments may conduct timeline-shift experiments, altering past events to change future outcomes; testing grounds require locations and objects capable of surviving extended timescales without substantial change while remaining inconspicuous—like forests with stone formations.

name: Deep Ecology Fascism
summary: An ideology asserting that nature possesses sentience and moral equivalence with humans, requiring legal protection from environmental harm.
evidence_for: If reincarnation exists, once humanity is eradicated, collective human souls inhabit new species, granting them human-level consciousness; humans may not be the first intelligent species on Earth, and future life may inherit our spiritual legacy.

name: Deathworm
summary: A legendary Mongolian creature described as a massive worm moving beneath sand and consuming lost travelers.
evidence_for: Ageless entities lacking mortality may develop vastly different instincts; a deathworm immune to aging and dominating its environment has no reproduction necessity for species survival, resulting in extreme rarity—possibly only one individual in existence.

name: KKK funded by governments
summary: A theory that the Ku Klux Klan and other white supremacist groups were perpetuated and funded by governments to suppress minority populations.
evidence_for: If governments desire population control, directing larger groups to target minorities through numerical superiority accomplishes reduction; humans can be divided into infinite categories, allowing this process to repeat indefinitely across multiple demographic axes.

name: Titanic Didn't Sink
summary: A claim that the Titanic was secretly swapped with the RMS Olympia before its voyage for insurance fraud purposes.
evidence_for: Massive structures like the Titanic fail unusually frequently; an alien or advanced force may prevent humanity from constructing excessively large entities like Dyson spheres, constraining human civilization to Earth by sabotaging super-structure projects.

name: Smithsonian Suppression
summary: An allegation that the Smithsonian Institution has destroyed or hidden human remains contradicting modern evolutionary theory.
evidence_for: Many organizations vehemently oppose concepts like giants or deities to preserve the narrative of human superiority; acknowledging advanced predecessors would undermine faith in human organizations and governments, threatening their authority and legitimacy.
